chapters.en: And Absalom rose up early, and stood beside the way of the gate: and it was , that when any man that had a controversy came to the king for judgment, then Absalom called unto him, and said, Of what city thou? And he said, Thy servant of one of the tribes of Israel.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙对他说：你的事有情有理，无奈王没有委人听你伸诉。
chapters.en: And Absalom said unto him, See, thy matters good and right; but no man of the king to hear thee.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙又说：恨不得我作国中的士师！凡有争讼求审判的到我这里来，我必秉公判断。
chapters.en: Absalom said moreover, Oh that I were made judge in the land, that every man which hath any suit or cause might come unto me, and I would do him justice!

chapters.zh: 若有人近前来要拜押沙龙，押沙龙就伸手拉住他，与他亲嘴。
chapters.en: And it was , that when any man came nigh to do him obeisance, he put forth his hand, and took him, and kissed him.

chapters.zh: 以色列人中，凡去见王求判断的，押沙龙都是如此待他们。这样，押沙龙暗中得了以色列人的心。
chapters.en: And on this manner did Absalom to all Israel that came to the king for judgment: so Absalom stole the hearts of the men of Israel.

chapters.zh: 满了四十年（有作四年的），押沙龙对王说：求你准我往希伯仑去，还我向耶和华所许的愿。
chapters.en: And it came to pass after forty years, that Absalom said unto the king, I pray thee, let me go and pay my vow, which I have vowed unto the LORD, in Hebron.

chapters.zh: 因为仆人住在亚兰的基述，曾许愿说：耶和华若使我再回耶路撒冷，我必事奉他。
chapters.en: For thy servant vowed a vow while I abode at Geshur in Syria, saying, If the LORD shall bring me again indeed to Jerusalem, then I will serve the LORD.

chapters.zh: 王说：你平平安安地去罢！押沙龙就起身，往希伯仑去了。
chapters.en: And the king said unto him, Go in peace. So he arose, and went to Hebron.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙打发探子走遍以色列各支派，说：你们一听见角声就说：押沙龙在希伯仑作王了！
chapters.en: But Absalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, As soon as ye hear the sound of the trumpet, then ye shall say, Absalom reigneth in Hebron.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙在耶路撒冷请了二百人与他同去，都是诚诚实实去的，并不知道其中的真情。
chapters.en: And with Absalom went two hundred men out of Jerusalem, called; and they went in their simplicity, and they knew not any thing.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙献祭的时候，打发人去将大卫的谋士、基罗人亚希多弗从他本城请了来。于是叛逆的势派甚大；因为随从押沙龙的人民，日渐增多。
chapters.en: And Absalom sent for Ahithophel the Gilonite, David's counsellor, from his city, from Giloh, while he offered sacrifices. And the conspiracy was strong; for the people increased continually with Absalom.

chapters.zh: 有人报告大卫说：以色列人的心都归向押沙龙了！
chapters.en: And there came a messenger to David, saying, The hearts of the men of Israel are after Absalom.

chapters.zh: 大卫就对耶路撒冷跟随他的臣仆说：我们要起来逃走，不然都不能躲避押沙龙了；要速速地去，恐怕他忽然来到，加害于我们，用刀杀尽合城的人。
chapters.en: And David said unto all his servants that with him at Jerusalem, Arise, and let us flee; for we shall not escape from Absalom: make speed to depart, lest he overtake us suddenly, and bring evil upon us, and smite the city with the edge of the sword.

chapters.zh: 王的臣仆对王说：我主我王所定的，仆人都愿遵行。
chapters.en: And the king's servants said unto the king, Behold, thy servants whatsoever my lord the king shall appoint.

chapters.zh: 于是王带著全家的人出去了，但留下十个妃嫔看守宫殿。
chapters.en: And the king went forth, and all his household after him. And the king left ten women, concubines, to keep the house.

chapters.zh: 王出去，众民都跟随他，到伯墨哈，就住下了。
chapters.en: And the king went forth, and all the people after him, and tarried in a place that was far off.

chapters.zh: 王的臣仆都在他面前过去。基利提人、比利提人，就是从迦特跟随王来的六百人，也都在他面前过去。
chapters.en: And all his servants passed on beside him; and all the Cherethites, and all the Pelethites, and all the Gittites, six hundred men which came after him from Gath, passed on before the king.

chapters.zh: 王对迦特人以太说：你是外邦逃来的人，为甚么与我们同去呢？你可以回去与新王同住，或者回你本地去罢！
chapters.en: Then said the king to Ittai the Gittite, Wherefore goest thou also with us? return to thy place, and abide with the king: for thou a stranger, and also an exile.

chapters.zh: 你来的日子不多，我今日怎好叫你与我们一同飘流、没有一定的住处呢？你不如带你的弟兄回去罢！愿耶和华用慈爱诚实待你。
chapters.en: Whereas thou camest yesterday, should I this day make thee go up and down with us? seeing I go whither I may, return thou, and take back thy brethren: mercy and truth with thee.

chapters.zh: 以太对王说：我指著永生的耶和华起誓，又敢在王面前起誓：无论生死，王在哪里，仆人也必在那里。
chapters.en: And Ittai answered the king, and said, the LORD liveth, and my lord the king liveth, surely in what place my lord the king shall be, whether in death or life, even there also will thy servant be.

chapters.zh: 大卫对以太说：你前去过河罢！于是迦特人以太带著跟随他的人和所有的妇人孩子，就都过去了。
chapters.en: And David said to Ittai, Go and pass over. And Ittai the Gittite passed over, and all his men, and all the little ones that with him.

chapters.zh: 本地的人都放声大哭。众民尽都过去，王也过了汲沦溪；众民往旷野去了。
chapters.en: And all the country wept with a loud voice, and all the people passed over: the king also himself passed over the brook Kidron, and all the people passed over, toward the way of the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 撒督和抬神约柜的利未人也一同来了，将神的约柜放下。亚比亚他上来，等著众民从城里出来过去。
chapters.en: And lo Zadok also, and all the Levites with him, bearing the ark of the covenant of God: and they set down the ark of God; and Abiathar went up, until all the people had done passing out of the city.

chapters.zh: 王对撒督说：你将神的约柜抬回城去。我若在耶和华眼前蒙恩，他必使我回来，再见约柜和他的居所。
chapters.en: And the king said unto Zadok, Carry back the ark of God into the city: if I shall find favour in the eyes of the LORD, he will bring me again, and shew me it, and his habitation:

chapters.zh: 倘若他说：我不喜悦你，看哪，我在这里，愿他凭自己的意旨待我！
chapters.en: But if he thus say, I have no delight in thee; behold, I, let him do to me as seemeth good unto him.

chapters.zh: 王又对祭司撒督说：你不是先见么？你可以安然回城；你儿子亚希玛斯和亚比亚他的儿子约拿单都可以与你同去。
chapters.en: The king said also unto Zadok the priest, thou a seer? return into the city in peace, and your two sons with you, Ahimaaz thy son, and Jonathan the son of Abiathar.

chapters.zh: 我在旷野的渡口那里等你们报信给我。
chapters.en: See, I will tarry in the plain of the wilderness, until there come word from you to certify me.

chapters.zh: 于是撒督和亚比亚他将神的约柜抬回耶路撒冷，他们就住在那里。
chapters.en: Zadok therefore and Abiathar carried the ark of God again to Jerusalem: and they tarried there.

chapters.zh: 大卫蒙头赤脚上橄榄山，一面上一面哭。跟随他的人也都蒙头哭著上去；
chapters.en: And David went up by the ascent of Olivet, and wept as he went up, and had his head covered, and he went barefoot: and all the people that with him covered every man his head, and they went up, weeping as they went up.

chapters.zh: 有人告诉大卫说：亚希多弗也在叛党之中，随从押沙龙。大卫祷告说：耶和华啊，求你使亚希多弗的计谋变为愚拙！
chapters.en: And told David, saying, Ahithophel among the conspirators with Absalom. And David said, O LORD, I pray thee, turn the counsel of Ahithophel into foolishness.

chapters.zh: 大卫到了山顶、敬拜神的地方，见亚基人户筛，衣服撕裂，头蒙灰尘来迎接他。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that David was come to the top , where he worshipped God, behold, Hushai the Archite came to meet him with his coat rent, and earth upon his head:

chapters.zh: 大卫对他说：你若与我同去，必累赘我；
chapters.en: Unto whom David said, If thou passest on with me, then thou shalt be a burden unto me:

chapters.zh: 你若回城去，对押沙龙说：王啊，我愿作你的仆人；我向来作你父亲的仆人，现在我也照样作你的仆人。这样，你就可以为我破坏亚希多弗的计谋。
chapters.en: But if thou return to the city, and say unto Absalom, I will be thy servant, O king; I thy father's servant hitherto, so I now also thy servant: then mayest thou for me defeat the counsel of Ahithophel.

chapters.zh: 祭司撒督和亚比亚他岂不都在那里么？你在王宫里听见甚么，就要告诉祭司撒督和亚比亚他。
chapters.en: And not there with thee Zadok and Abiathar the priests? therefore it shall be, what thing soever thou shalt hear out of the king's house, thou shalt tell to Zadok and Abiathar the priests.

chapters.zh: 撒督的儿子亚希玛斯，亚比亚他的儿子约拿单，也都在那里。凡你们所听见的可以托这二人来报告我。
chapters.en: Behold, there with them their two sons, Ahimaaz Zadok's , and Jonathan Abiathar's ; and by them ye shall send unto me every thing that ye can hear.

chapters.zh: 于是，大卫的朋友户筛进了城；押沙龙也进了耶路撒冷。
chapters.en: So Hushai David's friend came into the city, and Absalom came into Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 大卫刚过山顶，见米非波设的仆人洗巴拉著备好了的两匹驴，驴上驮著二百面饼，一百葡萄饼，一百个夏天的果饼，一皮袋酒来迎接他。
chapters.en: And when David was a little past the top , behold, Ziba the servant of Mephibosheth met him, with a couple of asses saddled, and upon them two hundred of bread, and an hundred bunches of raisins, and an hundred of summer fruits, and a bottle of wine.

chapters.zh: 王问洗巴说：你带这些来是甚么意思呢？洗巴说：驴是给王的家眷骑的；面饼和夏天的果饼是给少年人吃的；酒是给在旷野疲乏人喝的。
chapters.en: And the king said unto Ziba, What meanest thou by these? And Ziba said, The asses for the king's household to ride on; and the bread and summer fruit for the young men to eat; and the wine, that such as be faint in the wilderness may drink.

chapters.zh: 王问说：你主人的儿子在哪里呢？洗巴回答王说：他仍在耶路撒冷，因他说：以色列人今日必将我父的国归还我。
chapters.en: And the king said, And where thy master's son? And Ziba said unto the king, Behold, he abideth at Jerusalem: for he said, To day shall the house of Israel restore me the kingdom of my father.

chapters.zh: 王对洗巴说：凡属米非波设的都归你了。洗巴说：我叩拜我主我王，愿我在你眼前蒙恩。
chapters.en: Then said the king to Ziba, Behold, thine all that unto Mephibosheth. And Ziba said, I humbly beseech thee I may find grace in thy sight, my lord, O king.

chapters.zh: 大卫王到了巴户琳，见有一个人出来，是扫罗族基拉的儿子，名叫示每。他一面走一面咒骂，
chapters.en: And when king David came to Bahurim, behold, thence came out a man of the family of the house of Saul, whose name Shimei, the son of Gera: he came forth, and cursed still as he came.

chapters.zh: 又拿石头砍大卫王和王的臣仆；众民和勇士都在王的左右。
chapters.en: And he cast stones at David, and at all the servants of king David: and all the people and all the mighty men on his right hand and on his left.

chapters.zh: 示每咒骂说：你这流人血的坏人哪，去罢去罢！
chapters.en: And thus said Shimei when he cursed, Come out, come out, thou bloody man, and thou man of Belial:

chapters.zh: 你流扫罗全家的血，接续他作王；耶和华把这罪归在你身上，将这国交给你儿子押沙龙。现在你自取其祸，因为你是流人血的人。
chapters.en: The LORD hath returned upon thee all the blood of the house of Saul, in whose stead thou hast reigned; and the LORD hath delivered the kingdom into the hand of Absalom thy son: and, behold, thou in thy mischief, because thou a bloody man.

chapters.zh: 洗鲁雅的儿子亚比筛对王说：这死狗岂可咒骂我主我王呢？求你容我过去，割下他的头来。
chapters.en: Then said Abishai the son of Zeruiah unto the king, Why should this dead dog curse my lord the king? let me go over, I pray thee, and take off his head.

chapters.zh: 王说：洗鲁雅的儿子，我与你们有何关涉呢？他咒骂是因耶和华吩咐他说：你要咒骂大卫。如此，谁敢说你为甚么这样行呢？
chapters.en: And the king said, What have I to do with you, ye sons of Zeruiah? so let him curse, because the LORD hath said unto him, Curse David. Who shall then say, Wherefore hast thou done so?

chapters.zh: 大卫又对亚比筛和众臣仆说：我亲生的儿子尚且寻索我的性命，何况这便雅悯人呢？由他咒骂罢！因为这是耶和华吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And David said to Abishai, and to all his servants, Behold, my son, which came forth of my bowels, seeketh my life: how much more now Benjamite ? let him alone, and let him curse; for the LORD hath bidden him.

chapters.zh: 或者耶和华见我遭难，为我今日被这人咒骂，就施恩与我。
chapters.en: It may be that the LORD will look on mine affliction, and that the LORD will requite me good for his cursing this day.

chapters.zh: 于是大卫和跟随他的人往前行走。示每在大卫对面山坡，一面行走一面咒骂，又拿石头砍他，拿土扬他。
chapters.en: And as David and his men went by the way, Shimei went along on the hill's side over against him, and cursed as he went, and threw stones at him, and cast dust.

chapters.zh: 王和跟随他的众人疲疲乏乏地到了一个地方，就在那里歇息歇息。
chapters.en: And the king, and all the people that with him, came weary, and refreshed themselves there.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙和以色列众人来到耶路撒冷；亚希多弗也与他同来。
chapters.en: And Absalom, and all the people the men of Israel, came to Jerusalem, and Ahithophel with him.

chapters.zh: 大卫的朋友亚基人户筛去见押沙龙，对他说：愿王万岁！愿王万岁！
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when Hushai the Archite, David's friend, was come unto Absalom, that Hushai said unto Absalom, God save the king, God save the king.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙问户筛说：这是你恩待朋友么？为甚么不与你的朋友同去呢？
chapters.en: And Absalom said to Hushai, this thy kindness to thy friend? why wentest thou not with thy friend?

chapters.zh: 户筛对押沙龙说：不然，耶和华和这民，并以色列众人所拣选的，我必归顺他，与他同住。
chapters.en: And Hushai said unto Absalom, Nay; but whom the LORD, and this people, and all the men of Israel, choose, his will I be, and with him will I abide.

chapters.zh: 再者，我当服事谁呢？岂不是前王的儿子么？我怎样服事你父亲，也必照样服事你。
chapters.en: And again, whom should I serve? not in the presence of his son? as I have served in thy father's presence, so will I be in thy presence.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙对亚希多弗说：你们出个主意，我们怎样行才好？
chapters.en: Then said Absalom to Ahithophel, Give counsel among you what we shall do.

chapters.zh: 亚希多弗对押沙龙说：你父所留下看守宫殿的妃嫔，你可以与他们亲近。以色列众人听见你父亲憎恶你，凡归顺你人的手就更坚强。
chapters.en: And Ahithophel said unto Absalom, Go in unto thy father's concubines, which he hath left to keep the house; and all Israel shall hear that thou art abhorred of thy father: then shall the hands of all that with thee be strong.

chapters.zh: 于是人为押沙龙在宫殿的平顶上支搭帐棚；押沙龙在以色列众人眼前，与他父的妃嫔亲近。
chapters.en: So they spread Absalom a tent upon the top of the house; and Absalom went in unto his father's concubines in the sight of all Israel.

chapters.zh: 那时亚希多弗所出的主意好像人问神的话一样；他昔日给大卫，今日给押沙龙所出的主意，都是这样。
chapters.en: And the counsel of Ahithophel, which he counselled in those days, as if a man had enquired at the oracle of God: so all the counsel of Ahithophel both with David and with Absalom.

chapters.zh: 亚希多弗又对押沙龙说：求你准我挑选一万二千人，今夜我就起身追赶大卫，
chapters.en: Moreover Ahithophel said unto Absalom, Let me now choose out twelve thousand men, and I will arise and pursue after David this night:

chapters.zh: 趁他疲乏手软，我忽然追上他，使他惊惶；跟随他的民必都逃跑，我就单杀王一人，
chapters.en: And I will come upon him while he weary and weak handed, and will make him afraid: and all the people that with him shall flee; and I will smite the king only:

chapters.zh: 使众民都归顺你。你所寻找的人既然死了，众民就如已经归顺你；这样，也都平安无事了。
chapters.en: And I will bring back all the people unto thee: the man whom thou seekest as if all returned: all the people shall be in peace.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙和以色列的长老都以这话为美。
chapters.en: And the saying pleased Absalom well, and all the elders of Israel.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙说：要召亚基人户筛来，我们也要听他怎样说。
chapters.en: Then said Absalom, Call now Hushai the Archite also, and let us hear likewise what he saith.

chapters.zh: 户筛到了押沙龙面前，押沙龙向他说：亚希多弗是如此如此说的，我们照著他的话行可以不可以？若不可，你就说罢！
chapters.en: And when Hushai was come to Absalom, Absalom spake unto him, saying, Ahithophel hath spoken after this manner: shall we do his saying? if not; speak thou.

chapters.zh: 户筛对押沙龙说：亚希多弗这次所定的谋不善。
chapters.en: And Hushai said unto Absalom, The counsel that Ahithophel hath given not good at this time.

chapters.zh: 户筛又说：你知道，你父亲和跟随他的人都是勇士，现在他们心里恼怒，如同田野丢崽子的母熊一般，而且你父亲是个战士，必不和民一同住宿。
chapters.en: For, said Hushai, thou knowest thy father and his men, that they mighty men, and they chafed in their minds, as a bear robbed of her whelps in the field: and thy father a man of war, and will not lodge with the people.

chapters.zh: 他现今或藏在坑中或在别处，若有人首先被杀，凡听见的必说：跟随押沙龙的民被杀了。
chapters.en: Behold, he is hid now in some pit, or in some place: and it will come to pass, when some of them be overthrown at the first, that whosoever heareth it will say, There is a slaughter among the people that follow Absalom.

chapters.zh: 虽有人胆大如狮子，他的心也必消化；因为以色列人都知道你父亲是英雄，跟随他的人也都是勇士。
chapters.en: And he also valiant, whose heart as the heart of a lion, shall utterly melt: for all Israel knoweth that thy father a mighty man, and which with him valiant men.

chapters.zh: 依我之计，不如将以色列众人 ─ 从但直到别是巴，如同海边的沙那样多 ─ 聚集到你这里来，你也亲自率领他们出战。
chapters.en: Therefore I counsel that all Israel be generally gathered unto thee, from Dan even to Beersheba, as the sand that by the sea for multitude; and that thou go to battle in thine own person.

chapters.zh: 这样，我们在何处遇见他，就下到他那里，如同露水下在地上一般，连他带跟随他的人，一个也不留下。
chapters.en: So shall we come upon him in some place where he shall be found, and we will light upon him as the dew falleth on the ground: and of him and of all the men that with him there shall not be left so much as one.

chapters.zh: 他若进了哪一座城，以色列众人必带绳子去，将那城拉到河里，甚至连一块小石头都不剩下。
chapters.en: Moreover, if he be gotten into a city, then shall all Israel bring ropes to that city, and we will draw it into the river, until there be not one small stone found there.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙和以色列众人说：亚基人户筛的计谋比亚希多弗的计谋更好！这是因耶和华定意破坏亚希多弗的良谋，为要降祸与押沙龙。
chapters.en: And Absalom and all the men of Israel said, The counsel of Hushai the Archite better than the counsel of Ahithophel. For the LORD had appointed to defeat the good counsel of Ahithophel, to the intent that the LORD might bring evil upon Absalom.

chapters.zh: 户筛对祭司撒督和亚比亚他说：亚希多弗为押沙龙和以色列的长老所定的计谋是如此如此，我所定的计谋是如此如此。
chapters.en: Then said Hushai unto Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, Thus and thus did Ahithophel counsel Absalom and the elders of Israel; and thus and thus have I counselled.

chapters.zh: 现在你们要急速打发人去，告诉大卫说：今夜不可住在旷野的渡口，务要过河，免得王和跟随他的人都被吞灭。
chapters.en: Now therefore send quickly, and tell David, saying, Lodge not this night in the plains of the wilderness, but speedily pass over; lest the king be swallowed up, and all the people that with him.

chapters.zh: 那时，约拿单和亚希玛斯在隐罗结那里等候，不敢进城，恐怕被人看见。有一个使女出来，将这话告诉他们，他们就去报信给大卫王。
chapters.en: Now Jonathan and Ahimaaz stayed by Enrogel; for they might not be seen to come into the city: and a wench went and told them; and they went and told king David.

chapters.zh: 然而有一个童子看见他们，就去告诉押沙龙。他们急忙跑到巴户琳某人的家里；那人院中有一口井，他们就下到井里。
chapters.en: Nevertheless a lad saw them, and told Absalom: but they went both of them away quickly, and came to a man's house in Bahurim, which had a well in his court; whither they went down.

chapters.zh: 那家的妇人用盖盖上井口，又在上头铺上碎麦，事就没有泄漏。
chapters.en: And the woman took and spread a covering over the well's mouth, and spread ground corn thereon; and the thing was not known.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙的仆人来到那家，问妇人说：亚希玛斯和约拿单在哪里？妇人说：他们过了河了。仆人找他们，找不著，就回耶路撒冷去了。
chapters.en: And when Absalom's servants came to the woman to the house, they said, Where Ahimaaz and Jonathan? And the woman said unto them, They be gone over the brook of water. And when they had sought and could not find , they returned to Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 他们走后，二人从井里上来，去告诉大卫王说：亚希多弗如此如此定计害你，你们务要起来，快快过河。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, after they were departed, that they came up out of the well, and went and told king David, and said unto David, Arise, and pass quickly over the water: for thus hath Ahithophel counselled against you.

chapters.zh: 于是大卫和跟随他的人都起来，过约但河。到了天亮，无一人不过约但河的。
chapters.en: Then David arose, and all the people that with him, and they passed over Jordan: by the morning light there lacked not one of them that was not gone over Jordan.

chapters.zh: 亚希多弗见不依从他的计谋，就备上驴，归回本城；到了家，留下遗言，便吊死了，葬在他父亲的坟墓里。
chapters.en: And when Ahithophel saw that his counsel was not followed, he saddled ass, and arose, and gat him home to his house, to his city, and put his household in order, and hanged himself, and died, and was buried in the sepulchre of his father.

chapters.zh: 大卫到了玛哈念，押沙龙和跟随他的以色列人也都过了约但河。
chapters.en: Then David came to Mahanaim. And Absalom passed over Jordan, he and all the men of Israel with him.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙立亚玛撒作元帅，代替约押。亚玛撒是以实玛利人（又作以色列人）以特拉的儿子。以特拉曾与拿辖的女儿亚比该亲近；这亚比该与约押的母亲洗鲁雅是姊妹。
chapters.en: And Absalom made Amasa captain of the host instead of Joab: which Amasa a man's son, whose name Ithra an Israelite, that went in to Abigail the daughter of Nahash, sister to Zeruiah Joab's mother.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙和以色列人都安营在基列地。
chapters.en: So Israel and Absalom pitched in the land of Gilead.

chapters.zh: 大卫到了玛哈念，亚扪族的拉巴人拿辖的儿子朔比，罗底巴人亚米利的儿子玛吉，基列的罗基琳人巴西莱，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when David was come to Mahanaim, that Shobi the son of Nahash of Rabbah of the children of Ammon, and Machir the son of Ammiel of Lodebar, and Barzillai the Gileadite of Rogelim,

chapters.zh: 带著被、褥、盆、碗、瓦器、小麦、大麦、麦面、炒谷、豆子、红豆、炒豆、
chapters.en: Brought beds, and basons, and earthen vessels, and wheat, and barley, and flour, and parched , and beans, and lentiles, and parched ,

chapters.zh: 蜂蜜、奶油、绵羊、奶饼，供给大卫和跟随他的人吃；他们说：民在旷野，必饥渴困乏了。
chapters.en: And honey, and butter, and sheep, and cheese of kine, for David, and for the people that with him, to eat: for they said, The people hungry, and weary, and thirsty, in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 大卫数点跟随他的人，立千夫长、百夫长率领他们。
chapters.en: And David numbered the people that with him, and set captains of thousands and captains of hundreds over them.

chapters.zh: 大卫打发军兵出战，分为三队：一队在约押手下，一队在洗鲁雅的儿子、约押兄弟亚比筛手下，一队在迦特人以太手下。大卫对军兵说：我必与你们一同出战。
chapters.en: And David sent forth a third part of the people under the hand of Joab, and a third part under the hand of Abishai the son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother, and a third part under the hand of Ittai the Gittite. And the king said unto the people, I will surely go forth with you myself also.

chapters.zh: 军兵却说：你不可出战。若是我们逃跑，敌人必不介意；我们阵亡一半，敌人也不介意。因为你一人强似我们万人，你不如在城里预备帮助我们。
chapters.en: But the people answered, Thou shalt not go forth: for if we flee away, they will not care for us; neither if half of us die, will they care for us: but now worth ten thousand of us: therefore now better that thou succour us out of the city.

chapters.zh: 王向他们说：你们以为怎样好，我就怎样行。于是王站在城门旁，军兵或百或千地挨次出去了。
chapters.en: And the king said unto them, What seemeth you best I will do. And the king stood by the gate side, and all the people came out by hundreds and by thousands.

chapters.zh: 王嘱咐约押、亚比筛、以太说：你们要为我的缘故宽待那少年人押沙龙。王为押沙龙嘱咐众将的话，兵都听见了。
chapters.en: And the king commanded Joab and Abishai and Ittai, saying, gently for my sake with the young man, with Absalom. And all the people heard when the king gave all the captains charge concerning Absalom.

chapters.zh: 兵就出到田野迎著以色列人，在以法莲树林里交战。
chapters.en: So the people went out into the field against Israel: and the battle was in the wood of Ephraim;

chapters.zh: 以色列人败在大卫的仆人面前；那日阵亡的甚多，共有二万人。
chapters.en: Where the people of Israel were slain before the servants of David, and there was there a great slaughter that day of twenty thousand .

chapters.zh: 因为在那里四面打仗，死于树林的比死于刀剑的更多。
chapters.en: For the battle was there scattered over the face of all the country: and the wood devoured more people that day than the sword devoured.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙偶然遇见大卫的仆人。押沙龙骑著骡子，从大橡树密枝底下经过，他的头发被树枝绕住，就悬挂起来，所骑的骡子便离他去了。
chapters.en: And Absalom met the servants of David. And Absalom rode upon a mule, and the mule went under the thick boughs of a great oak, and his head caught hold of the oak, and he was taken up between the heaven and the earth; and the mule that under him went away.

chapters.zh: 有个人看见，就告诉约押说：我看见押沙龙挂在橡树上了。
chapters.en: And a certain man saw , and told Joab, and said, Behold, I saw Absalom hanged in an oak.

chapters.zh: 约押对报信的人说：你既看见他，为甚么不将他打死落在地上呢？你若打死他，我就赏你十舍客勒银子，一条带子。
chapters.en: And Joab said unto the man that told him, And, behold, thou sawest , and why didst thou not smite him there to the ground? and I would have given thee ten of silver, and a girdle.

chapters.zh: 那人对约押说：我就是得你一千舍客勒银子，我也不敢伸手害王的儿子；因为我们听见王嘱咐你和亚比筛并以太说：你们要谨慎，不可害那少年人押沙龙。
chapters.en: And the man said unto Joab, Though I should receive a thousand of silver in mine hand, would I not put forth mine hand against the king's son: for in our hearing the king charged thee and Abishai and Ittai, saying, Beware that none the young man Absalom.

chapters.zh: 我若妄为害了他的性命，就是你自己也必与我为敌（原来，无论何事都瞒不过王。）
chapters.en: Otherwise I should have wrought falsehood against mine own life: for there is no matter hid from the king, and thou thyself wouldest have set thyself against .

chapters.zh: 约押说：我不能与你留连。约押手拿三杆短枪，趁押沙龙在橡树上还活著，就刺透他的心。
chapters.en: Then said Joab, I may not tarry thus with thee. And he took three darts in his hand, and thrust them through the heart of Absalom, while he yet alive in the midst of the oak.

chapters.zh: 给约押拿兵器的十个少年人围绕押沙龙，将他杀死。
chapters.en: And ten young men that bare Joab's armour compassed about and smote Absalom, and slew him.

chapters.zh: 约押吹角，拦阻众人，他们就回来，不再追赶以色列人。
chapters.en: And Joab blew the trumpet, and the people returned from pursuing after Israel: for Joab held back the people.

chapters.zh: 他们将押沙龙丢在林中一个大坑里，上头堆起一大堆石头。以色列众人都逃跑，各回各家去了。
chapters.en: And they took Absalom, and cast him into a great pit in the wood, and laid a very great heap of stones upon him: and all Israel fled every one to his tent.

chapters.zh: 押沙龙活著的时候，在王谷立了一根石柱，因他说：我没有儿子为我留名。他就以自己的名称那石柱叫押沙龙柱，直到今日。
chapters.en: Now Absalom in his lifetime had taken and reared up for himself a pillar, which in the king's dale: for he said, I have no son to keep my name in remembrance: and he called the pillar after his own name: and it is called unto this day, Absalom's place.

chapters.zh: 撒督的儿子亚希玛斯说：容我跑去，将耶和华向仇敌给王报仇的信息报与王知。
chapters.en: Then said Ahimaaz the son of Zadok, Let me now run, and bear the king tidings, how that the LORD hath avenged him of his enemies.

chapters.zh: 约押对他说：你今日不可去报信，改日可以报信；因为今日王的儿子死了，所以你不可去报信。
chapters.en: And Joab said unto him, Thou shalt not bear tidings this day, but thou shalt bear tidings another day: but this day thou shalt bear no tidings, because the king's son is dead.

chapters.zh: 约押对古示人说：你去将你所看见的告诉王。古示人在约押面前下拜，就跑去了。
chapters.en: Then said Joab to Cushi, Go tell the king what thou hast seen. And Cushi bowed himself unto Joab, and ran.

chapters.zh: 撒督的儿子亚希玛斯又对约押说：无论怎样，求你容我随著古示人跑去。约押说：我儿，你报这信息，既不得赏赐，何必要跑去呢？
chapters.en: Then said Ahimaaz the son of Zadok yet again to Joab, But howsoever, let me, I pray thee, also run after Cushi. And Joab said, Wherefore wilt thou run, my son, seeing that thou hast no tidings ready?

chapters.zh: 他又说：无论怎样，我要跑去。约押说：你跑去罢！亚希玛斯就从平原往前跑，跑过古示人去了。
chapters.en: But howsoever, , let me run. And he said unto him, Run. Then Ahimaaz ran by the way of the plain, and overran Cushi.

chapters.zh: 大卫正坐在城瓮里。守望的人上城门楼的顶上，举目观看，见有一个人独自跑来。
chapters.en: And David sat between the two gates: and the watchman went up to the roof over the gate unto the wall, and lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold a man running alone.

chapters.zh: 守望的人就大声告诉王。王说：他若独自来，必是报口信的。那人跑得渐渐近了。
chapters.en: And the watchman cried, and told the king. And the king said, If he alone, tidings in his mouth. And he came apace, and drew near.

chapters.zh: 守望的人又见一人跑来，就对守城门的人说：又有一人独自跑来。王说：这也必是报信的。
chapters.en: And the watchman saw another man running: and the watchman called unto the porter, and said, Behold man running alone. And the king said, He also bringeth tidings.

chapters.zh: 守望的人说：我看前头人的跑法，好像撒督的儿子亚希玛斯的跑法一样。王说：他是个好人，必是报好信息。
chapters.en: And the watchman said, Me thinketh the running of the foremost is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok. And the king said, He a good man, and cometh with good tidings.

chapters.zh: 亚希玛斯向王呼叫说：平安了！就在王面前脸伏于地叩拜，说：耶和华 ─ 你的神是应当称颂的，因他已将那举手攻击我主我王的人交给王了。
chapters.en: And Ahimaaz called, and said unto the king, All is well. And he fell down to the earth upon his face before the king, and said, Blessed the LORD thy God, which hath delivered up the men that lifted up their hand against my lord the king.

chapters.zh: 王问说：少年人押沙龙平安不平安？亚希玛斯回答说：约押打发王的仆人，那时仆人听见众民大声喧哗，却不知道是甚么事。
chapters.en: And the king said, Is the young man Absalom safe? And Ahimaaz answered, When Joab sent the king's servant, and thy servant, I saw a great tumult, but I knew not what .

chapters.zh: 王说：你退去，站在旁边。他就退去，站在旁边。
chapters.en: And the king said , Turn aside, stand here. And he turned aside, and stood still.

chapters.zh: 古示人也来到，说：有信息报给我主我王！耶和华今日向一切兴起攻击你的人给你报仇了。
chapters.en: And, behold, Cushi came; and Cushi said, Tidings, my lord the king: for the LORD hath avenged thee this day of all them that rose up against thee.

chapters.zh: 王问古示人说：少年人押沙龙平安不平安？古示人回答说：愿我主我王的仇敌，和一切兴起要杀害你的人，都与那少年人一样。
chapters.en: And the king said unto Cushi, Is the young man Absalom safe? And Cushi answered, The enemies of my lord the king, and all that rise against thee to do hurt, be as young man .

chapters.zh: 王就心里伤恸，上城门楼去哀哭，一面走一面说：我儿押沙龙啊！我儿，我儿押沙龙啊！我恨不得替你死，押沙龙啊，我儿！我儿！
chapters.en: And the king was much moved, and went up to the chamber over the gate, and wept: and as he went, thus he said, O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! would God I had died for thee, O Absalom, my son, my son!

chapters.zh: 有人告诉约押说：王为押沙龙哭泣悲哀。
chapters.en: And it was told Joab, Behold, the king weepeth and mourneth for Absalom.

chapters.zh: 众民听说王为他儿子忧愁，他们得胜的欢乐却变成悲哀。
chapters.en: And the victory that day was into mourning unto all the people: for the people heard say that day how the king was grieved for his son.

chapters.zh: 那日众民暗暗地进城，就如败阵逃跑、惭愧的民一般。
chapters.en: And the people gat them by stealth that day into the city, as people being ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.

chapters.zh: 王蒙著脸，大声哭号说：我儿押沙龙啊！押沙龙，我儿，我儿啊！
chapters.en: But the king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!

chapters.zh: 约押进去见王，说：你今日使你一切仆人脸面惭愧了！他们今日救了你的性命和你儿女妻妾的性命，
chapters.en: And Joab came into the house to the king, and said, Thou hast shamed this day the faces of all thy servants, which this day have saved thy life, and the lives of thy sons and of thy daughters, and the lives of thy wives, and the lives of thy concubines;

chapters.zh: 你却爱那恨你的人，恨那爱你的人。你今日明明地不以将帅、仆人为念。我今日看明，若押沙龙活著，我们都死亡，你就喜悦了。
chapters.en: In that thou lovest thine enemies, and hatest thy friends. For thou hast declared this day, that thou regardest neither princes nor servants: for this day I perceive, that if Absalom had lived, and all we had died this day, then it had pleased thee well.

chapters.zh: 现在你当出去，安慰你仆人的心。我指著耶和华起誓：你若不出去，今夜必无一人与你同在一处；这祸患就比你从幼年到如今所遭的更甚！
chapters.en: Now therefore arise, go forth, and speak comfortably unto thy servants: for I swear by the LORD, if thou go not forth, there will not tarry one with thee this night: and that will be worse unto thee than all the evil that befell thee from thy youth until now.

chapters.zh: 于是王起来，坐在城门口。众民听说王坐在城门口，就都到王面前。以色列人已经逃跑，各回各家 . 去了。
chapters.en: Then the king arose, and sat in the gate. And they told unto all the people, saying, Behold, the king doth sit in the gate. And all the people came before the king: for Israel had fled every man to his tent.

chapters.zh: 以色列众支派的人纷纷议论说：王曾救我们脱离仇敌的手，又救我们脱离非利士人的手，现在他躲避押沙龙逃走了。
chapters.en: And all the people were at strife throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, The king saved us out of the hand of our enemies, and he delivered us out of the hand of the Philistines; and now he is fled out of the land for Absalom.

chapters.zh: 我们膏押沙龙治理我们，他已经阵亡。现在为甚么不出一言请王回来呢？
chapters.en: And Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why speak ye not a word of bringing the king back?

chapters.zh: 大卫王差人去见祭司撒督和亚比亚他，说：你们当向犹大长老说：以色列众人已经有话请王回宫，你们为甚么落在他们后头呢？
chapters.en: And king David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, saying, Speak unto the elders of Judah, saying, Why are ye the last to bring the king back to his house? seeing the speech of all Israel is come to the king, to his house.

chapters.zh: 你们是我的弟兄，是我的骨肉，为甚么在人后头请王回来呢？
chapters.en: Ye my brethren, ye my bones and my flesh: wherefore then are ye the last to bring back the king?

chapters.zh: 也要对亚玛撒说：你不是我的骨肉么？我若不立你替约押常作元帅，愿神重重地降罚与我！
chapters.en: And say ye to Amasa, thou not of my bone, and of my flesh? God do so to me, and more also, if thou be not captain of the host before me continually in the room of Joab.

chapters.zh: 如此就挽回犹大众人的心，如同一人的心。他们便打发人去见王，说：请王和王的一切臣仆回来。
chapters.en: And he bowed the heart of all the men of Judah, even as one man; so that they sent unto the king, Return thou, and all thy servants.

chapters.zh: 王就回来，到了约但河。犹大人来到吉甲，要去迎接王，请他过约但河。
chapters.en: So the king returned, and came to Jordan. And Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king, to conduct the king over Jordan.

chapters.zh: 巴户琳的便雅悯人、基拉的儿子示每急忙与犹大人一同下去迎接大卫王。
chapters.en: And Shimei the son of Gera, a Benjamite, which of Bahurim, hasted and came down with the men of Judah to meet king David.

chapters.zh: 跟从示每的有一千便雅悯人，还有扫罗家的仆人洗巴和他十五个儿子，二十个仆人；他们都逿过约但河迎接王。
chapters.en: And a thousand men of Benjamin with him, and Ziba the servant of the house of Saul, and his fifteen sons and his twenty servants with him; and they went over Jordan before the king.

chapters.zh: 有摆渡船过去，渡王的家眷，任王使用。王要过约但河的时候，基拉的儿子示每就俯伏在王面前，
chapters.en: And there went over a ferry boat to carry over the king's household, and to do what he thought good. And Shimei the son of Gera fell down before the king, as he was come over Jordan;

chapters.zh: 对王说：我主我王出耶路撒冷的时候，仆人行悖逆的事，现在求我主不要因此加罪与仆人，不要记念，也不要放在心上。
chapters.en: And said unto the king, Let not my lord impute iniquity unto me, neither do thou remember that which thy servant did perversely the day that my lord the king went out of Jerusalem, that the king should take it to his heart.

chapters.zh: 仆人明知自己有罪，所以约瑟全家之中，今日我首先下来迎接我主我王。
chapters.en: For thy servant doth know that I have sinned: therefore, behold, I am come the first this day of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.

chapters.zh: 洗鲁雅的儿子亚比筛说：示每既咒骂耶和华的受膏者，不应当治死他么？
chapters.en: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered and said, Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD'S anointed?

chapters.zh: 大卫说：洗鲁雅的儿子，我与你们有何关涉，使你们今日与我反对呢？今日在以色列中岂可治死人呢？我岂不知今日我作以色列的王么？
chapters.en: And David said, What have I to do with you, ye sons of Zeruiah, that ye should this day be adversaries unto me? shall there any man be put to death this day in Israel? for do not I know that I this day king over Israel?

chapters.zh: 于是王对示每说：你必不死。王就向他起誓。
chapters.en: Therefore the king said unto Shimei, Thou shalt not die. And the king sware unto him.

chapters.zh: 扫罗的孙子米非波设也下去迎接王。他自从王去的日子，直到王平平安安地回来，没有修脚，没有剃胡须，也没有洗衣服。
chapters.en: And Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king, and had neither dressed his feet, nor trimmed his beard, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came in peace.

chapters.zh: 他来到耶路撒冷迎接王的时候，王问他说：米非波设，你为甚么没有与我同去呢？
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when he was come to Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said unto him, Wherefore wentest not thou with me, Mephibosheth?

chapters.zh: 他回答说：我主我王，仆人是瘸腿的。那日我想要备驴骑上，与王同去，无奈我的仆人欺哄了我，
chapters.en: And he answered, My lord, O king, my servant deceived me: for thy servant said, I will saddle me an ass, that I may ride thereon, and go to the king; because thy servant lame.

chapters.zh: 又在我主我王面前谗毁我。然而我主我王如同神的使者一般，你看怎样好，就怎样行罢！
chapters.en: And he hath slandered thy servant unto my lord the king; but my lord the king as an angel of God: do therefore good in thine eyes.

chapters.zh: 因为我祖全家的人，在我主我王面前都算为死人，王却使仆人在王的席上同人吃饭，我现在向王还能辨理诉冤么？
chapters.en: For all my father's house were but dead men before my lord the king: yet didst thou set thy servant among them that did eat at thine own table. What right therefore have I yet to cry any more unto the king?

chapters.zh: 王对他说：你何必再提你的事呢？我说，你与洗巴均分地土。
chapters.en: And the king said unto him, Why speakest thou any more of thy matters? I have said, Thou and Ziba divide the land.

chapters.zh: 米非波设对王说：我主我王既平平安安地回宫，就任凭洗巴都取了也可以。
chapters.en: And Mephibosheth said unto the king, Yea, let him take all, forasmuch as my lord the king is come again in peace unto his own house.

chapters.zh: 基列人巴西莱从罗基琳下来，要送王过约但河，就与王一同过了约但河。
chapters.en: And Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim, and went over Jordan with the king, to conduct him over Jordan.

chapters.zh: 巴西莱年纪老迈，已经八十岁了。王住在玛哈念的时候，他就拿食物来供给王；他原是大富户。
chapters.en: Now Barzillai was a very aged man, fourscore years old: and he had provided the king of sustenance while he lay at Mahanaim; for he a very great man.

chapters.zh: 王对巴西莱说：你与我同去，我要在耶路撒冷那里养你的老。
chapters.en: And the king said unto Barzillai, Come thou over with me, and I will feed thee with me in Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 巴西莱对王说：我在世的年日还能有多少，使我与王同上耶路撒冷呢？
chapters.en: And Barzillai said unto the king, How long have I to live, that I should go up with the king unto Jerusalem?

chapters.zh: 仆人现在八十岁了，还能尝出饮食的滋味、辨别美恶么？还能听男女歌唱的声音么？仆人何必累赘我主我王呢？
chapters.en: I this day fourscore years old: can I discern between good and evil? can thy servant taste what I eat or what I drink? can I hear any more the voice of singing men and singing women? wherefore then should thy servant be yet a burden unto my lord the king?

chapters.zh: 仆人只要送王过约但河，王何必赐我这样的恩典呢？
chapters.en: Thy servant will go a little way over Jordan with the king: and why should the king recompense it me with such a reward?

chapters.zh: 求你准我回去，好死在我本城，葬在我父母的墓旁。这里有王的仆人金罕，让他同我主我王过去，可以随意待他。
chapters.en: Let thy servant, I pray thee, turn back again, that I may die in mine own city, by the grave of my father and of my mother. But behold thy servant Chimham; let him go over with my lord the king; and do to him what shall seem good unto thee.

chapters.zh: 王说：金罕可以与我同去，我必照你的心愿待他。你向我求甚么，我都必为你成就。
chapters.en: And the king answered, Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do to him that which shall seem good unto thee: and whatsoever thou shalt require of me, will I do for thee.

chapters.zh: 于是众民过约但河，王也过去。王与巴西莱亲嘴，为他祝福，巴西莱就回本地去了。
chapters.en: And all the people went over Jordan. And when the king was come over, the king kissed Barzillai, and blessed him; and he returned unto his own place.

chapters.zh: 王过去，到了吉甲，金罕也跟他过去。犹大众民和以色列民的一半也都送王过去。
chapters.en: Then the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him: and all the people of Judah conducted the king, and also half the people of Israel.

chapters.zh: 以色列众人来见王，对他说：我们弟兄犹大人为甚么暗暗送王和王的家眷，并跟随王的人过约但河？
chapters.en: And, behold, all the men of Israel came to the king, and said unto the king, Why have our brethren the men of Judah stolen thee away, and have brought the king, and his household, and all David's men with him, over Jordan?

chapters.zh: 犹大众人回答以色列人说：因为王与我们是亲属，你们为何因这事发怒呢？我们吃了王的甚么呢？王赏赐了我们甚么呢？
chapters.en: And all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, Because the king near of kin to us: wherefore then be ye angry for this matter? have we eaten at all of the king's ? or hath he given us any gift?

chapters.zh: 以色列人回答犹大人说：按支派，我们与王有十分的情分；在大卫身上，我们也比你们更有情分。你们为何藐视我们，请王回来不先与我们商量呢？但犹大人的话比以色列人的话更硬。
chapters.en: And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, and said, We have ten parts in the king, and we have also more in David than ye: why then did ye despise us, that our advice should not be first had in bringing back our king? And the words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel.

chapters.zh: 在那里恰巧有一个匪徒，名叫示巴，是便雅悯人比基利的儿子。他吹角，说：我们与大卫无分，与耶西的儿子无涉。以色列人哪，你们各回各家去罢！
chapters.en: And there happened to be there a man of Belial, whose name Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite: and he blew a trumpet, and said, We have no part in David, neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse: every man to his tents, O Israel.

chapters.zh: 于是以色列人都离开大卫，跟随比基利的儿子示巴。但犹大人从约但河直到耶路撒冷，都紧紧跟随他们的王。
chapters.en: So every man of Israel went up from after David, followed Sheba the son of Bichri: but the men of Judah clave unto their king, from Jordan even to Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 大卫王来到耶路撒冷，进了宫殿，就把从前留下看守宫殿的十个妃嫔禁闭在冷宫，养活他们，不与他们亲近。他们如同寡妇被禁，直到死的日子。
chapters.en: And David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women concubines, whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in ward, and fed them, but went not in unto them. So they were shut up unto the day of their death, living in widowhood.

chapters.zh: 王对亚玛撒说：你要在三日之内将犹大人招聚了来，你也回到这里来。
chapters.en: Then said the king to Amasa, Assemble me the men of Judah within three days, and be thou here present.

chapters.zh: 亚玛撒就去招聚犹大人，却耽延过了王所限的日期。
chapters.en: So Amasa went to assemble Judah: but he tarried longer than the set time which he had appointed him.

chapters.zh: 大卫对亚比筛说：现在恐怕比基利的儿子示巴加害于我们比押沙龙更甚。你要带领你主的仆人追赶他，免得他得了坚固城，躲避我们。
chapters.en: And David said to Abishai, Now shall Sheba the son of Bichri do us more harm than Absalom: take thou thy lord's servants, and pursue after him, lest he get him fenced cities, and escape us.

chapters.zh: 约押的人和基利提人、比利提人，并所有的勇士，都跟著亚比筛，从耶路撒冷出去追赶比基利的儿子示巴。
chapters.en: And there went out after him Joab's men, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and all the mighty men: and they went out of Jerusalem, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.

chapters.zh: 他们到了基遍的大磐石那里，亚玛撒来迎接他们。那时约押穿著战衣，腰束佩刀的带子，刀在鞘内；约押前行，刀从鞘内掉出来。
chapters.en: When they at the great stone which in Gibeon, Amasa went before them. And Joab's garment that he had put on was girded unto him, and upon it a girdle a sword fastened upon his loins in the sheath thereof; and as he went forth it fell out.

chapters.zh: 约押左手拾起刀来，对亚玛撒说：我兄弟，你好啊！就用右手抓住亚玛撒的胡子，要与他亲嘴。
chapters.en: And Joab said to Amasa, thou in health, my brother? And Joab took Amasa by the beard with the right hand to kiss him.

chapters.zh: 亚玛撒没有防备约押手里所拿的刀；约押用刀刺入他的肚腹，他的肠子流在地上，没有再刺他，就死了。约押和他兄弟亚比筛往前追赶比基利的儿子示巴。
chapters.en: But Amasa took no heed to the sword that in Joab's hand: so he smote him therewith in the fifth , and shed out his bowels to the ground, and struck him not again; and he died. So Joab and Abishai his brother pursued after Sheba the son of Bichri.

chapters.zh: 有约押的一个少年人站在亚玛撒尸身旁边，对众人说：谁喜悦约押，谁归顺大卫，就当跟随约押去。
chapters.en: And one of Joab's men stood by him, and said, He that favoureth Joab, and he that for David, after Joab.

chapters.zh: 亚玛撒在道路上滚在自己的血里。那人见众民经过都站住，就把亚玛撒的尸身从路上挪到田间，用衣服遮盖。
chapters.en: And Amasa wallowed in blood in the midst of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people stood still, he removed Amasa out of the highway into the field, and cast a cloth upon him, when he saw that every one that came by him stood still.

chapters.zh: 尸身从路上挪移之后，众民就都跟随约押去追赶比基利的儿子示巴。
chapters.en: When he was removed out of the highway, all the people went on after Joab, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.

chapters.zh: 他走遍以色列各支派，直到伯玛迦的亚比拉，并比利人的全地；那些地方的人也都聚集跟随他。
chapters.en: And he went through all the tribes of Israel unto Abel, and to Bethmaachah, and all the Berites: and they were gathered together, and went also after him.

chapters.zh: 约押和跟随的人到了伯玛迦的亚比拉，围困示巴，就对著城筑垒；跟随约押的众民用锤撞城，要使城塌陷。
chapters.en: And they came and besieged him in Abel of Bethmaachah, and they cast up a bank against the city, and it stood in the trench: and all the people that with Joab battered the wall, to throw it down.

chapters.zh: 有一个聪明妇人从城上呼叫说：听啊，听啊，请约押近前来，我好与他说话。
chapters.en: Then cried a wise woman out of the city, Hear, hear; say, I pray you, unto Joab, Come near hither, that I may speak with thee.

chapters.zh: 约押就近前来，妇人问他说：你是约押不是？他说：我是。妇人说：求你听婢女的话。约押说：我听。
chapters.en: And when he was come near unto her, the woman said, thou Joab? And he answered, I . Then she said unto him, Hear the words of thine handmaid. And he answered, I do hear.

chapters.zh: 妇人说：古时有话说，当先在亚比拉求问，然后事就定妥。
chapters.en: Then she spake, saying, They were wont to speak in old time, saying, They shall surely ask at Abel: and so they ended .

chapters.zh: 我们这城的人在以色列人中是和平、忠厚的。你为何要毁坏以色列中的大城，吞灭耶和华的产业呢？
chapters.en: I peaceable faithful in Israel: thou seekest to destroy a city and a mother in Israel: why wilt thou swallow up the inheritance of the LORD?

chapters.zh: 约押回答说：我决不吞灭毁坏，
chapters.en: And Joab answered and said, Far be it, far be it from me, that I should swallow up or destroy.

chapters.zh: 乃因以法莲山地的一个人 ─ 比基利的儿子示巴 ─ 举手攻击大卫王，你们若将他一人交出来，我便离城而去。妇人对约押说：那人的首级必从城墙上丢给你。
chapters.en: The matter not so: but a man of mount Ephraim, Sheba the son of Bichri by name, hath lifted up his hand against the king, against David: deliver him only, and I will depart from the city. And the woman said unto Joab, Behold, his head shall be thrown to thee over the wall.

chapters.zh: 妇人就凭他的智慧去劝众人。他们便割下比基利的儿子示巴的首级，丢给约押。约押吹角，众人就离城而散，各归各家去了。约押回耶路撒冷，到王那里。
chapters.en: Then the woman went unto all the people in her wisdom. And they cut off the head of Sheba the son of Bichri, and cast out to Joab. And he blew a trumpet, and they retired from the city, every man to his tent. And Joab returned to Jerusalem unto the king.

chapters.zh: 约押作以色列全军的元帅；耶何耶大的儿子比拿雅统辖基利提人和比利提人；
chapters.en: Now Joab over all the host of Israel: and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada over the Cherethites and over the Pelethites:

chapters.zh: 亚多兰掌管服苦的人；亚希律的儿子约沙法作史官；
chapters.en: And Adoram over the tribute: and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud recorder:

chapters.zh: 示法作书记；撒督和亚比亚他作祭司长；
chapters.en: And Sheva scribe: and Zadok and Abiathar the priests:

chapters.zh: 睚珥人以拉作大卫的宰相。
chapters.en: And Ira also the Jairite was a chief ruler about David.

chapters.zh: 大卫年间有饥荒，一连三年，大卫就求问耶和华。耶和华说：这饥荒是因扫罗和他流人血之家杀死基遍人。
chapters.en: Then there was a famine in the days of David three years, year after year; and David enquired of the LORD. And the LORD answered, for Saul, and for bloody house, because he slew the Gibeonites.

chapters.zh: 原来这基遍人不是以色列人，乃是亚摩利人中所剩的；以色列人曾向他们起誓，不杀灭他们，扫罗却为以色列人和犹大人发热心，想要杀灭他们。大卫王召了他们来，
chapters.en: And the king called the Gibeonites, and said unto them; (now the Gibeonites not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; and the children of Israel had sworn unto them: and Saul sought to slay them in his zeal to the children of Israel and Judah.)

chapters.zh: 问他们说：我当为你们怎样行呢？可用甚么赎这罪，使你们为耶和华的产业祝福呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore David said unto the Gibeonites, What shall I do for you? and wherewith shall I make the atonement, that ye may bless the inheritance of the LORD?

chapters.zh: 基遍人回答说：我们和扫罗与他家的事并不关乎金银，也不要因我们的缘故杀一个以色列人。大卫说：你们怎样说，我就为你们怎样行。
chapters.en: And the Gibeonites said unto him, We will have no silver nor gold of Saul, nor of his house; neither for us shalt thou kill any man in Israel. And he said, What ye shall say, will I do for you.

chapters.zh: 他们对王说：那从前谋害我们、要灭我们、使我们不得再住以色列境内的人，
chapters.en: And they answered the king, The man that consumed us, and that devised against us we should be destroyed from remaining in any of the coasts of Israel,

chapters.zh: 现在愿将他的子孙七人交给我们，我们好在耶和华面前，将他们悬挂在耶和华拣选扫罗的基比亚。王说：我必交给你们。
chapters.en: Let seven men of his sons be delivered unto us, and we will hang them up unto the LORD in Gibeah of Saul, the LORD did choose. And the king said, I will give .

chapters.zh: 王因为曾与扫罗的儿子约拿单指著耶和华起誓结盟，就爱惜扫罗的孙子、约拿单的儿子米非波设，不交出来，
chapters.en: But the king spared Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan the son of Saul, because of the LORD'S oath that between them, between David and Jonathan the son of Saul.

chapters.zh: 却把爱雅的女儿利斯巴给扫罗所生的两个儿子亚摩尼、米非波设，和扫罗女儿米甲的姊姊给米何拉人巴西莱儿子亚得列所生的五个儿子
chapters.en: But the king took the two sons of Rizpah the daughter of Aiah, whom she bare unto Saul, Armoni and Mephibosheth; and the five sons of Michal the daughter of Saul, whom she brought up for Adriel the son of Barzillai the Meholathite:

chapters.zh: 交在基遍人的手里。基遍人就把他们，在耶和华面前，悬挂在山上，这七人就一同死亡。被杀的时候正是收割的日子，就是动手割大麦的时候。
chapters.en: And he delivered them into the hands of the Gibeonites, and they hanged them in the hill before the LORD: and they fell seven together, and were put to death in the days of harvest, in the first , in the beginning of barley harvest.

chapters.zh: 爱雅的女儿利斯巴用麻布在磐石上搭棚，从动手收割的时候直到天降雨在尸身上的时候，日间不容空中的雀鸟落在尸身上，夜间不让田野的走兽前来糟践。
chapters.en: And Rizpah the daughter of Aiah took sackcloth, and spread it for her upon the rock, from the beginning of harvest until water dropped upon them out of heaven, and suffered neither the birds of the air to rest on them by day, nor the beasts of the field by night.

chapters.zh: 有人将扫罗的妃嫔爱雅女儿利斯巴所行的这事告诉大卫。
chapters.en: And it was told David what Rizpah the daughter of Aiah, the concubine of Saul, had done.

chapters.zh: 大卫就去，从基列雅比人那里将扫罗和他儿子约拿单的骸骨搬了来（是因非利士人从前在基利波杀扫罗，将尸身悬挂在伯珊的街市上，基列雅比人把尸身偷了去。）
chapters.en: And David went and took the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son from the men of Jabeshgilead, which had stolen them from the street of Bethshan, where the Philistines had hanged them, when the Philistines had slain Saul in Gilboa:

chapters.zh: 大卫将扫罗和他儿子约拿单的骸骨从那里搬了来，又收殓被悬挂七人的骸骨，
chapters.en: And he brought up from thence the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son; and they gathered the bones of them that were hanged.

chapters.zh: 将扫罗和他儿子约拿单的骸骨葬在便雅悯的洗拉，在扫罗父亲基士的坟墓里；众人行了王所吩咐的。此后神垂听国民所求的。
chapters.en: And the bones of Saul and Jonathan his son buried they in the country of Benjamin in Zelah, in the sepulchre of Kish his father: and they performed all that the king commanded. And after that God was intreated for the land.

chapters.zh: 非利士人与以色列人打仗；大卫带领仆人下去，与非利士人接战，大卫就疲乏了。
chapters.en: Moreover the Philistines had yet war again with Israel; and David went down, and his servants with him, and fought against the Philistines: and David waxed faint.

chapters.zh: 伟人的一个儿子以实比诺要杀大卫；他的铜枪重三百舍客勒，又佩著新刀。
chapters.en: And Ishbibenob, which of the sons of the giant, the weight of whose spear three hundred of brass in weight, he being girded with a new , thought to have slain David.

chapters.zh: 但洗鲁雅的儿子亚比筛帮助大卫，攻打非利士人，将他杀死。当日，跟随大卫的人向大卫起誓说：以后你不可再与我们一同出战，恐怕熄灭以色列的灯。
chapters.en: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah succoured him, and smote the Philistine, and killed him. Then the men of David sware unto him, saying, Thou shalt go no more out with us to battle, that thou quench not the light of Israel.

chapters.zh: 后来，以色列人在歌伯与非利士人打仗，户沙人西比该杀了伟人的一个儿子撒弗。
chapters.en: And it came to pass after this, that there was again a battle with the Philistines at Gob: then Sibbechai the Hushathite slew Saph, which of the sons of the giant.

chapters.zh: 又在歌伯与非利士人打仗，伯利恒人雅雷俄珥金的儿子伊勒哈难杀了迦特人歌利亚。这人的枪杆粗如织布的机轴。
chapters.en: And there was again a battle in Gob with the Philistines, where Elhanan the son of Jaareoregim, a Bethlehemite, slew Goliath the Gittite, the staff of whose spear like a weaver's beam.

chapters.zh: 又在迦特打仗，那里有一个身量高大的人，手脚都是六指，共有二十四个指头；他也是伟人的儿子。
chapters.en: And there was yet a battle in Gath, where was a man of stature, that had on every hand six fingers, and on every foot six toes, four and twenty in number; and he also was born to the giant.

chapters.zh: 这人向以色列人骂阵，大卫的哥哥示米亚的儿子约拿单就杀了他。
chapters.en: And when he defied Israel, Jonathan the son of Shimea the brother of David slew him.

chapters.zh: 这四个人是迦特伟人的儿子，都死在大卫和他仆人的手下。
chapters.en: These four were born to the giant in Gath, and fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.

chapters.zh: 当耶和华救大卫脱离一切仇敌和扫罗之手的日子，他向耶和华念这诗，
chapters.en: And David spake unto the LORD the words of this song in the day the LORD had delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of Saul:

chapters.zh: 说：耶和华是我的岩石，我的山寨，我的救主，
chapters.en: And he said, The LORD my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer;

chapters.zh: 我的神，我的磐石，我所投靠的。他是我的盾牌，是拯救我的角，是我的高台，是我的避难所。我的救主啊，你是救我脱离强暴的。
chapters.en: The God of my rock; in him will I trust: my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower, and my refuge, my saviour; thou savest me from violence.

chapters.zh: 我要求告当赞美的耶和华，这样，我必从仇敌手中被救出来。
chapters.en: I will call on the LORD, worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 曾有死亡的波浪环绕我，匪类的急流使我惊惧，
chapters.en: When the waves of death compassed me, the floods of ungodly men made me afraid;

chapters.zh: 阴间的绳索缠绕我，死亡的网罗临到我。
chapters.en: The sorrows of hell compassed me about; the snares of death prevented me;

chapters.zh: 我在急难中求告耶和华，向我的神呼求。他从殿中听了我的声音；我的呼求入了他的耳中。
chapters.en: In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry into his ears.

chapters.zh: 那时因他发怒，地就摇撼战抖；天的根基也震动摇撼。
chapters.en: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations of heaven moved and shook, because he was wroth.

chapters.zh: 从他鼻孔冒烟上腾；从他口中发火焚烧，连炭也著了。
chapters.en: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.

chapters.zh: 他又使天下垂，亲自降临；有黑云在他脚下。
chapters.en: He bowed the heavens also, and came down; and darkness under his feet.

chapters.zh: 他坐著基路伯飞行，在风的翅膀上显现。
chapters.en: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: and he was seen upon the wings of the wind.

chapters.zh: 他以黑暗和聚集的水、天空的厚云为他四围的行宫。
chapters.en: And he made darkness pavilions round about him, dark waters, thick clouds of the skies.

chapters.zh: 因他面前的光辉炭都著了。
chapters.en: Through the brightness before him were coals of fire kindled.

chapters.zh: 耶和华从天上打雷；至高者发出声音。
chapters.en: The LORD thundered from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice.

chapters.zh: 他射出箭来，使仇敌四散，发出闪电，使他们扰乱。
chapters.en: And he sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的斥责一发，鼻孔的气一出，海底就出现，大地的根基也显露。
chapters.en: And the channels of the sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discovered, at the rebuking of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.

chapters.zh: 他从高天伸手抓住我，把我从大水中拉上来。
chapters.en: He sent from above, he took me; he drew me out of many waters;

chapters.zh: 他救我脱离我的劲敌和那些恨我的人，因为他们比我强盛。
chapters.en: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from them that hated me: for they were too strong for me.

chapters.zh: 我遭遇灾难的日子，他们来攻击我；但耶和华是我的倚靠。
chapters.en: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.

chapters.zh: 他又领我到宽阔之处；他救拔我，因他喜悦我。
chapters.en: He brought me forth also into a large place: he delivered me, because he delighted in me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华按著我的公义报答我，按著我手中的清洁赏赐我。
chapters.en: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness: according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.

chapters.zh: 因为我遵守了耶和华的道，未曾作恶离开我的神。
chapters.en: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

chapters.zh: 他的一切典章常在我面前；他的律例，我也未曾离弃。
chapters.en: For all his judgments before me: and his statutes, I did not depart from them.

chapters.zh: 我在他面前作了完全人；我也保守自己远离我的罪孽。
chapters.en: I was also upright before him, and have kept myself from mine iniquity.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华按我的公义，按我在他眼前的清洁赏赐我。
chapters.en: Therefore the LORD hath recompensed me according to my righteousness; according to my cleanness in his eye sight.

chapters.zh: 慈爱的人，你以慈爱待他；完全的人，你以完全待他；
chapters.en: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful, with the upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright.

chapters.zh: 清洁的人，你以清洁待他；乖僻的人，你以弯曲待他。
chapters.en: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury.

chapters.zh: 困苦的百姓，你必拯救；但你的眼目察看高傲的人，使他降卑。
chapters.en: And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes upon the haughty, thou mayest bring down.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你是我的灯；耶和华必照明我的黑暗。
chapters.en: For thou my lamp, O LORD: and the LORD will lighten my darkness.

chapters.zh: 我藉著你冲入敌军，藉著我的神跳过墙垣。
chapters.en: For by thee I have run through a troop: by my God have I leaped over a wall.

chapters.zh: 至于神，他的道是完全的；耶和华的话是炼净的。凡投靠他的，他便作他们的盾牌。
chapters.en: God, his way perfect; the word of the LORD tried: he a buckler to all them that trust in him.

chapters.zh: 除了耶和华，谁是神呢？除了我们的神，谁是磐石呢？
chapters.en: For who God, save the LORD? and who a rock, save our God?

chapters.zh: 神是我坚固的保障；他引导完全人行他的路。
chapters.en: God my strength power: and he maketh my way perfect.

chapters.zh: 他使我的脚快如母鹿的蹄，又使我在高处安稳。
chapters.en: He maketh my feet like hinds' : and setteth me upon my high places.

chapters.zh: 他教导我的手能以争战，甚至我的膀臂能开铜弓。
chapters.en: He teacheth my hands to war; so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.

chapters.zh: 你把你的救恩给我作盾牌；你的温和使我为大。
chapters.en: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy gentleness hath made me great.

chapters.zh: 你使我脚下的地步宽阔；我的脚未曾滑跌。
chapters.en: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.

chapters.zh: 我追赶我的仇敌，灭绝了他们，未灭以先，我没有归回。
chapters.en: I have pursued mine enemies, and destroyed them; and turned not again until I had consumed them.

chapters.zh: 我灭绝了他们，打伤了他们，使他们不能起来；他们都倒在我的脚下。
chapters.en: And I have consumed them, and wounded them, that they could not arise: yea, they are fallen under my feet.

chapters.zh: 因为你曾以力量束我的腰，使我能争战；你也使那起来攻击我的都服在我以下。
chapters.en: For thou hast girded me with strength to battle: them that rose up against me hast thou subdued under me.

chapters.zh: 你又使我的仇敌在我面前转背逃跑，叫我能以剪除那恨我的人。
chapters.en: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.

chapters.zh: 他们仰望，却无人拯救；就是呼求耶和华，他也不应允。
chapters.en: They looked, but none to save; unto the LORD, but he answered them not.

chapters.zh: 我捣碎他们，如同地上的灰尘，践踏他们，四散在地，如同街上的泥土。
chapters.en: Then did I beat them as small as the dust of the earth, I did stamp them as the mire of the street, did spread them abroad.

chapters.zh: 你救我脱离我百姓的争竞，保护我作列国的元首；我素不认识的民必事奉我。
chapters.en: Thou also hast delivered me from the strivings of my people, thou hast kept me head of the heathen: a people I knew not shall serve me.

chapters.zh: 外邦人要投降我，一听见我的名声就必顺从我。
chapters.en: Strangers shall submit themselves unto me: as soon as they hear, they shall be obedient unto me.

chapters.zh: 外邦人要衰残，战战兢兢地出他们的营寨。
chapters.en: Strangers shall fade away, and they shall be afraid out of their close places.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是活神，愿我的磐石被人称颂！愿神 ─ 那拯救我的磐石被人尊崇！
chapters.en: The LORD liveth; and blessed my rock; and exalted be the God of the rock of my salvation.

chapters.zh: 这位神就是那为我伸冤、使众民服在我以下的。
chapters.en: It God that avengeth me, and that bringeth down the people under me,

chapters.zh: 你救我脱离仇敌，又把我举起，高过那些起来攻击我的；你救我脱离强暴的人。
chapters.en: And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，因此我要在外邦中称谢你，歌颂你的名。
chapters.en: Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.

chapters.zh: 耶和华赐极大的救恩给他所立的王，施慈爱给他的受膏者，就是给大卫和他的后裔，直到永远！
chapters.en: the tower of salvation for his king: and sheweth mercy to his anointed, unto David, and to his seed for evermore.

chapters.zh: 以下是大卫末了的话。耶西的儿子大卫得居高位，是雅各神所膏的，作以色列的美歌者，说：
chapters.en: Now these the last words of David. David the son of Jesse said, and the man raised up on high, the anointed of the God of Jacob, and the sweet psalmist of Israel, said,

chapters.zh: 耶和华的灵藉著我说：他的话在我口中。
chapters.en: The Spirit of the LORD spake by me, and his word in my tongue.

chapters.zh: 以色列的神、以色列的磐石晓谕我说：那以公义治理人民的，敬畏神执掌权柄，
chapters.en: The God of Israel said, the Rock of Israel spake to me, He that ruleth over men just, ruling in the fear of God.

chapters.zh: 他必像日出的晨光，如无云的清晨，雨后的晴光，使地发生嫩草。
chapters.en: And as the light of the morning, the sun riseth, a morning without clouds; the tender grass out of the earth by clear shining after rain.

chapters.zh: 我家在神面前并非如此；神却与我立永远的约。这约凡事坚稳，关乎我的一切救恩和我一切所想望的，他岂不为我成就么？
chapters.en: Although my house not so with God; yet he hath made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all , and sure: for all my salvation, and all desire, although he make not to grow.

chapters.zh: 但匪类都必像荆棘被丢弃；人不敢用手拿他；
chapters.en: But of Belial all of them as thorns thrust away, because they cannot be taken with hands:

chapters.zh: 拿他的人必带铁器和枪杆，终久他必被火焚烧。
chapters.en: But the man shall touch them must be fenced with iron and the staff of a spear; and they shall be utterly burned with fire in the place.

chapters.zh: 大卫勇士的名字记在下面：他革扪人约设巴设，又称伊斯尼人亚底挪，他是军长的统领，一时击杀了八百人。
chapters.en: These the names of the mighty men whom David had: The Tachmonite that sat in the seat, chief among the captains; the same Adino the Eznite: against eight hundred, whom he slew at one time.

chapters.zh: 其次是亚合人朵多的儿子以利亚撒。从前非利士人聚集要打仗，以色列人迎著上去，有跟随大卫的三个勇士向非利士人骂阵，其中有以利亚撒。
chapters.en: And after him Eleazar the son of Dodo the Ahohite, of the three mighty men with David, when they defied the Philistines were there gathered together to battle, and the men of Israel were gone away:

chapters.zh: 他起来击杀非利士人，直到手臂疲乏，手黏住刀把。那日耶和华使以色列人大获全胜；众民在以利亚撒后头专夺财物。
chapters.en: He arose, and smote the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand clave unto the sword: and the LORD wrought a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to spoil.

chapters.zh: 其次是哈拉人亚基的儿子沙玛。一日，非利士人聚集成群，在一块长满红豆的田里，众民就在非利士人面前逃跑。
chapters.en: And after him Shammah the son of Agee the Hararite. And the Philistines were gathered together into a troop, where was a piece of ground full of lentiles: and the people fled from the Philistines.

chapters.zh: 沙玛却站在那田间击杀非利士人，救护了那田。耶和华使以色列人大获全胜。
chapters.en: But he stood in the midst of the ground, and defended it, and slew the Philistines: and the LORD wrought a great victory.

chapters.zh: 收割的时候，有三十个勇士中的三个人下到亚杜兰洞见大卫。非利士的军兵在利乏音谷安营。
chapters.en: And three of the thirty chief went down, and came to David in the harvest time unto the cave of Adullam: and the troop of the Philistines pitched in the valley of Rephaim.

chapters.zh: 那时大卫在山寨，非利士人的防营在伯利恒。
chapters.en: And David then in an hold, and the garrison of the Philistines then Bethlehem.

chapters.zh: 大卫渴想，说：甚愿有人将伯利恒城门旁、井里的水打来给我喝。
chapters.en: And David longed, and said, Oh that one would give me drink of the water of the well of Bethlehem, which by the gate!

chapters.zh: 这三个勇士就闯过非利士人的营盘，从伯利恒城门旁的井里打水，拿来奉给大卫。他却不肯喝，将水奠在耶和华面前，
chapters.en: And the three mighty men brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that by the gate, and took , and brought to David: nevertheless he would not drink thereof, but poured it out unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 说：耶和华啊，这三个人冒死去打水；这水好像他们的血一般，我断不敢喝。如此，大卫不肯喝。这是三个勇士所做的事。
chapters.en: And he said, Be it far from me, O LORD, that I should do this: the blood of the men that went in jeopardy of their lives? therefore he would not drink it. These things did these three mighty men.

chapters.zh: 洗鲁雅的儿子、约押的兄弟亚比筛是这三个勇士的首领；他举枪杀了三百人，就在三个勇士里得了名。
chapters.en: And Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief among three. And he lifted up his spear against three hundred, slew , and had the name among three.

chapters.zh: 他在这三个勇士里是最尊贵的，所以作他们的首领，只是不及前三个勇士。
chapters.en: Was he not most honourable of three? therefore he was their captain: howbeit he attained not unto the three.

chapters.zh: 有甲薛勇士耶何耶大的儿子比拿雅行过大能的事；他杀了摩押人亚利伊勒的两个儿子，又在下雪的时候下坑里去，杀了一个狮子，
chapters.en: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man, of Kabzeel, who had done many acts, he slew two lionlike men of Moab: he went down also and slew a lion in the midst of a pit in time of snow:

chapters.zh: 又杀了一个强壮的埃及人；埃及人手里拿著枪，比拿雅只拿著棍子下去，从埃及人手里夺过枪来，用那枪将他杀死。
chapters.en: And he slew an Egyptian, a goodly man: and the Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian's hand, and slew him with his own spear.

chapters.zh: 这是耶何耶大的儿子比拿雅所行的事，就在三个勇士里得了名。
chapters.en: These did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and had the name among three mighty men.

chapters.zh: 他比那三十个勇士都尊贵，只是不及前三个勇士。大卫立他作护卫长。
chapters.en: He was more honourable than the thirty, but he attained not to the three. And David set him over his guard.

chapters.zh: 三十个勇士里有约押的兄弟亚撒黑，伯利恒人朵多的儿子伊勒哈难，
chapters.en: Asahel the brother of Joab one of the thirty; Elhanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem,

chapters.zh: 哈律人沙玛，哈律人以利加，
chapters.en: Shammah the Harodite, Elika the Harodite,

chapters.zh: 帕勒提人希利斯，提哥亚人益吉的儿子以拉，
chapters.en: Helez the Paltite, Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite,

chapters.zh: 亚拿突人亚比以谢，户沙人米本乃，
chapters.en: Abiezer the Anethothite, Mebunnai the Hushathite,

chapters.zh: 亚合人撒们，尼陀法人玛哈莱，
chapters.en: Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite,

chapters.zh: 尼陀法人巴拿的儿子希立，便雅悯族、基比亚人利拜的儿子以太，
chapters.en: Heleb the son of Baanah, a Netophathite, Ittai the son of Ribai out of Gibeah of the children of Benjamin,

chapters.zh: 比拉顿人比拿雅，迦实溪人希太，
chapters.en: Benaiah the Pirathonite, Hiddai of the brooks of Gaash,

chapters.zh: 伯亚拉巴人亚比亚本，巴鲁米人押斯玛弗，
chapters.en: Abialbon the Arbathite, Azmaveth the Barhumite,

chapters.zh: 沙本人以利雅哈巴，雅善儿子中的约拿单，
chapters.en: Eliahba the Shaalbonite, of the sons of Jashen, Jonathan,

chapters.zh: 哈拉人沙玛，哈拉人沙拉的儿子亚希暗，
chapters.en: Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam the son of Sharar the Hararite,

chapters.zh: 玛迦人亚哈拜的儿子以利法列，基罗人亚希多弗的儿子以连，
chapters.en: Eliphelet the son of Ahasbai, the son of the Maachathite, Eliam the son of Ahithophel the Gilonite,

chapters.zh: 迦密人希斯莱，亚巴人帕莱，
chapters.en: Hezrai the Carmelite, Paarai the Arbite,

chapters.zh: 琐巴人拿单的儿子以甲，迦得人巴尼，
chapters.en: Igal the son of Nathan of Zobah, Bani the Gadite,

chapters.zh: 亚扪人洗勒，比录人拿哈莱（是给洗鲁雅的儿子约押拿兵器的），
chapters.en: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite, armourbearer to Joab the son of Zeruiah,

chapters.zh: 以帖人以拉，以帖人迦立，
chapters.en: Ira an Ithrite, Gareb an Ithrite,

chapters.zh: 赫人乌利亚，共有三十七人。
chapters.en: Uriah the Hittite: thirty and seven in all.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又向以色列人发怒，就激动大卫，使他吩咐人去数点以色列人和犹大人。
chapters.en: And again the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, and he moved David against them to say, Go, number Israel and Judah.

chapters.zh: 大卫就吩咐跟随他的元帅约押说：你去走遍以色列众支派，从但直到别是巴，数点百姓，我好知道他们的数目。
chapters.en: For the king said to Joab the captain of the host, which with him, Go now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even to Beersheba, and number ye the people, that I may know the number of the people.

chapters.zh: 约押对王说：无论百姓多少，愿耶和华 ─ 你的神再加增百倍，使我主我王亲眼得见。我主我王何必喜悦行这事呢？
chapters.en: And Joab said unto the king, Now the LORD thy God add unto the people, how many soever they be, an hundredfold, and that the eyes of my lord the king may see : but why doth my lord the king delight in this thing?

chapters.zh: 但王的命令胜过约押和众军长。约押和众军长就从王面前出去，数点以色列的百姓。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding the king's word prevailed against Joab, and against the captains of the host. And Joab and the captains of the host went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他们过了约但河，在迦得谷中、城的右边亚罗珥安营，与雅谢相对，
chapters.en: And they passed over Jordan, and pitched in Aroer, on the right side of the city that in the midst of the river of Gad, and toward Jazer:

chapters.zh: 又到了基列和他停合示地，又到了但雅安，绕到西顿，
chapters.en: Then they came to Gilead, and to the land of Tahtimhodshi; and they came to Danjaan, and about to Zidon,

chapters.zh: 来到推罗的保障，并希未人和迦南人的各城，又到犹大南方的别是巴。
chapters.en: And came to the strong hold of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites: and they went out to the south of Judah, to Beersheba.

chapters.zh: 他们走遍全地，过了九个月零二十天，就回到耶路撒冷。
chapters.en: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.

chapters.zh: 约押将百姓的总数奏告于王：以色列拿刀的勇士有八十万；犹大有五十万。
chapters.en: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people unto the king: and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword; and the men of Judah five hundred thousand men.

chapters.zh: 大卫数点百姓以后，就心中自责，祷告耶和华说：我行这事大有罪了。耶和华啊，求你除掉仆人的罪孽，因我所行的甚是愚昧。
chapters.en: And David's heart smote him after that he had numbered the people. And David said unto the LORD, I have sinned greatly in that I have done: and now, I beseech thee, O LORD, take away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.

chapters.zh: 大卫早晨起来，耶和华的话临到先知迦得，就是大卫的先见，说：
chapters.en: For when David was up in the morning, the word of the LORD came unto the prophet Gad, David's seer, saying,

chapters.zh: 你去告诉大卫，说耶和华如此说：我有三样灾，随你选择一样，我好降与你。
chapters.en: Go and say unto David, Thus saith the LORD, I offer thee three ; choose thee one of them, that I may unto thee.

chapters.zh: 于是迦得来见大卫，对他说：你愿意国中有七年的饥荒呢？是在你敌人面前逃跑，被追赶三个月呢？是在你国中有三日的瘟疫呢？现在你要揣摩思想，我好回覆那差我来的。
chapters.en: So Gad came to David, and told him, and said unto him, Shall seven years of famine come unto thee in thy land? or wilt thou flee three months before thine enemies, while they pursue thee? or that there be three days' pestilence in thy land? now advise, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.

chapters.zh: 大卫对迦得说：我甚为难！我愿落在耶和华的手里，因为他有丰盛的怜悯。我不愿落在人的手里。
chapters.en: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let us fall now into the hand of the LORD; for his mercies great: and let me not fall into the hand of man.

chapters.zh: 于是，耶和华降瘟疫与以色列人，自早晨到所定的时候；从但直到别是巴，民间死了七万人。
chapters.en: So the LORD sent a pestilence upon Israel from the morning even to the time appointed: and there died of the people from Dan even to Beersheba seventy thousand men.

chapters.zh: 天使向耶路撒冷伸手要灭城的时候，耶和华后悔，就不降这灾了，吩咐灭民的天使说：够了！住手罢！那时耶和华的使者在耶布斯人亚劳拿的禾场那里。
chapters.en: And when the angel stretched out his hand upon Jerusalem to destroy it, the LORD repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed the people, It is enough: stay now thine hand. And the angel of the LORD was by the threshingplace of Araunah the Jebusite.

chapters.zh: 大卫看见灭民的天使，就祷告耶和华说：我犯了罪，行了恶；但这群羊做了甚么呢？愿你的手攻击我和我的父家。
chapters.en: And David spake unto the LORD when he saw the angel that smote the people, and said, Lo, I have sinned, and I have done wickedly: but these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, be against me, and against my father's house.

chapters.zh: 当日，迦得来见大卫，对他说：你上去，在耶布斯人亚劳拿的禾场上为耶和华筑一座坛。
chapters.en: And Gad came that day to David, and said unto him, Go up, rear an altar unto the LORD in the threshingfloor of Araunah the Jebusite.

chapters.zh: 大卫就照著迦得奉耶和华名所说的话上去了。
chapters.en: And David, according to the saying of Gad, went up as the LORD commanded.

chapters.zh: 亚劳拿观看，见王和他臣仆前来，就迎接出去，脸伏于地，向王下拜，
chapters.en: And Araunah looked, and saw the king and his servants coming on toward him: and Araunah went out, and bowed himself before the king on his face upon the ground.

chapters.zh: 说：我主我王为何来到仆人这里呢？大卫说：我要买你这禾场，为耶和华筑一座坛，使民间的瘟疫止住。
chapters.en: And Araunah said, Wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said, To buy the threshingfloor of thee, to build an altar unto the LORD, that the plague may be stayed from the people.

chapters.zh: 亚劳拿对大卫说：我主我王，你喜悦用甚么，就拿去献祭。看哪，这里有牛可以作燔祭，有打粮的器具和套牛的轭可以当柴烧。
chapters.en: And Araunah said unto David, Let my lord the king take and offer up what good unto him: behold, oxen for burnt sacrifice, and threshing instruments and instruments of the oxen for wood.

chapters.zh: 王啊，这一切，我亚劳拿都奉给你；又对王说：愿耶和华 ─ 你的神悦纳你。
chapters.en: All these did Araunah, a king, give unto the king. And Araunah said unto the king, The LORD thy God accept thee.

chapters.zh: 王对亚劳拿说：不然。我必要按著价值向你买；我不肯用白得之物作燔祭献给耶和华 ─ 我的神。大卫就用五十舍客勒银子买了那禾场与牛。
chapters.en: And the king said unto Araunah, Nay; but I will surely buy of thee at a price: neither will I offer burnt offerings unto the LORD my God of that which doth cost me nothing. So David bought the threshingfloor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.

chapters.zh: 大卫在那里为耶和华筑了一座坛，献燔祭和平安祭。如此，耶和华垂听国民所求的，瘟疫在以色列人中就止住了。
chapters.en: And David built there an altar unto the LORD, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. So the LORD was intreated for the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
